{{redirect|Ushimaki|For the district in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, see {{ill|Ushimaki (Nagoya)|ja|牛牧 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimaki (Nagoya)|1=Ushimaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97218216}}}}  
'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸国牧]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11632062]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{redirect|Ushimaki|For the district in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, see {{ill|Ushimaki (Nagoya)|ja|牛牧 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimaki (Nagoya)|1=Ushimaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97218216}}}}  
'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

== Pastures and Locations ==

=== Shokoku Maki (Provincial Pastures) ===
* '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Shumon Horse Pasture|ja|朱門馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumon Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Shida Horse Pasture|ja|信太馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takatsu Horse Pasture|ja|高津馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Oiyui Horse Pasture|ja|大結馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiyui Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏見御厨|1=Oiyui Horse Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430131}}, {{ill|Kijima Horse Pasture|ja|木島馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kijima Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagasu Horse Grounds|ja|長洲馬場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Horse Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ukishima Cattle Grounds|ja|浮島牛場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Cattle Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Ōno Horse Pasture|ja|大野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Onoya Cattle Pasture|ja|負野牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onoya Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Araya Pasture|ja|新屋牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araya Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Awa Province (Chiba)|Awa Province (Japan)]]'''  
** {{ill|Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja|白浜馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kozu Horse Pasture|ja|粉師馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|高野馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Okano Horse Pasture|ja|岡野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okano Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Somi Horse Pasture|ja|蘇弥馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somi Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|長嶋馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Kintō Maki (Capital-Adjacent Pastures) ===
* '''[[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Torigai Pasture|ja|鳥養牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torigai Pasture|1=Torigai Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986435}}, {{ill|Toyoshima Pasture|ja|豊島牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshima Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inano Pasture|ja|為奈野牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inano Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Kōga Pasture|ja|甲賀牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōga Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Goma Pasture|ja|胡麻牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goma Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Tarumi Pasture|ja|垂水牧 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸国牧]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11632062]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{redirect|Ushimaki|For the district in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, see {{ill|Ushimaki (Nagoya)|ja|牛牧 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimaki (Nagoya)|1=Ushimaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97218216}}}}  
'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

== Pastures and Locations ==

=== Shokoku Maki (Provincial Pastures) ===
* '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Shumon Horse Pasture|ja|朱門馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumon Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Shida Horse Pasture|ja|信太馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takatsu Horse Pasture|ja|高津馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Oiyui Horse Pasture|ja|大結馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiyui Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏見御厨|1=Oiyui Horse Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430131}}, {{ill|Kijima Horse Pasture|ja|木島馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kijima Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagasu Horse Grounds|ja|長洲馬場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Horse Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ukishima Cattle Grounds|ja|浮島牛場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Cattle Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Ōno Horse Pasture|ja|大野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Onoya Cattle Pasture|ja|負野牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onoya Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Araya Pasture|ja|新屋牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araya Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Awa Province (Chiba)|Awa Province (Japan)]]'''  
** {{ill|Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja|白浜馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kozu Horse Pasture|ja|粉師馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|高野馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Okano Horse Pasture|ja|岡野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okano Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Somi Horse Pasture|ja|蘇弥馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somi Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|長嶋馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Kintō Maki (Capital-Adjacent Pastures) ===
* '''[[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Torigai Pasture|ja|鳥養牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torigai Pasture|1=Torigai Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986435}}, {{ill|Toyoshima Pasture|ja|豊島牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshima Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inano Pasture|ja|為奈野牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inano Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Kōga Pasture|ja|甲賀牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōga Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Goma Pasture|ja|胡麻牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goma Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Tarumi Pasture|ja|垂水牧 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'''
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}

According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. - Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. {{redirect|Ushimaki|For the district in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, see {{ill|Ushimaki (Nagoya)|ja|牛牧 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimaki (Nagoya)|1=Ushimaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97218216}}}}  
'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸国牧]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸国牧]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諸国牧]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632062]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{redirect|Ushimaki|For the district in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, see {{ill|Ushimaki (Nagoya)|ja|牛牧 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimaki (Nagoya)|1=Ushimaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97218216}}}}  
'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

== Pastures and Locations ==

=== Shokoku Maki (Provincial Pastures) ===
* '''[[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Shumon Horse Pasture|ja|朱門馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumon Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Shida Horse Pasture|ja|信太馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takatsu Horse Pasture|ja|高津馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Oiyui Horse Pasture|ja|大結馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiyui Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 夏見御厨|1=Oiyui Horse Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430131}}, {{ill|Kijima Horse Pasture|ja|木島馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kijima Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagasu Horse Grounds|ja|長洲馬場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Horse Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ukishima Cattle Grounds|ja|浮島牛場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukishima Cattle Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Ōno Horse Pasture|ja|大野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Onoya Cattle Pasture|ja|負野牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onoya Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Araya Pasture|ja|新屋牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Araya Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Awa Province (Chiba)|Awa Province (Japan)]]'''  
** {{ill|Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja|白浜馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kozu Horse Pasture|ja|粉師馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|高野馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Okano Horse Pasture|ja|岡野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okano Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Somi Horse Pasture|ja|蘇弥馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somi Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|長嶋馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Kintō Maki (Capital-Adjacent Pastures) ===
* '''[[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Torigai Pasture|ja|鳥養牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torigai Pasture|1=Torigai Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986435}}, {{ill|Toyoshima Pasture|ja|豊島牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshima Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inano Pasture|ja|為奈野牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inano Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Kōga Pasture|ja|甲賀牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōga Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Tanba Province|Tanba Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Goma Pasture|ja|胡麻牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goma Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Tarumi Pasture|ja|垂水牧 (播磨国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'''

'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

== Pastures and Locations ==

* '''[[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]'''

* '''[[:en:Awa Province (Chiba)|Awa Province (Japan)]]'''  
** {{ill|Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja|白浜馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kozu Horse Pasture|ja|粉師馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|高野馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]'''  
** {{ill|Okano Horse Pasture|ja|岡野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okano Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Somi Horse Pasture|ja|蘇弥馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somi Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''[[:en:Bizen Province|Bizen Province]]'''

=== Kintō Maki (Capital-Adjacent Pastures) ===

According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]] in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for [[:en:Horses in warfare|warhorses]], horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]].  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ([[:en:Saikaidō|Saikaidō]]) were sent to the [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu (Japan)]].  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]] and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial governor]] supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier [[:en:Soldier|soldiers]] cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{redirect|Ushimaki|For the district in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, see {{ill|Ushimaki (Nagoya)|ja|牛牧 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimaki (Nagoya)|1=Ushimaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97218216}}}}  
'''{{ill|Shokoku Maki|ja|諸国牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokoku Maki|qq=}}''' (しょこくまき) refers to the pastures developed and managed by the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}} in ancient Japan. These pastures primarily served as breeding grounds for {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|WD=Q1194462|lt=warhorses}}, horses for the aristocracy ({{ill|tribute horses|ja|貢馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tribute horses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|post horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/post horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381453}} for relay stations. Additionally, to accommodate horses and cattle gathered from the Shokoku Maki, separate pastures called '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were established in Kyushu and around the Kinai region for free-range grazing. This article covers both Shokoku Maki and Kintō Maki.

* Apart from these official pastures, the {{ill|Imperial Stables Bureau|ja|内厩寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Stables Bureau|1=Imperial Stables Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60985176}} also managed imperial pastures known as {{ill|Chokushi Maki|ja|勅旨牧|1=Chokushi Maki|2=ja|3=勅旨牧|4=zh|5=勅旨牧|6=fr|7=Chokushi Maki|8=de|9=Chokushi Maki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushi Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11399928}}.

== Overview ==
The exact time when the '''Shokoku Maki''' and '''Kintō Maki''' were established is unclear, but they are believed to have originated in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}.  
Shokoku Maki were pastures under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, there were 24 horse pastures in 18 provinces, 12 cattle pastures, and 3 combined horse and cattle pastures, totaling 39 official pastures. Each year, five-year-old horses from these pastures were sent to the {{ill|Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja|左右馬寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left and Right Stables Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while those from the pastures in the western provinces ({{ill|Saikaidō|en|Saikaidō|WD=Q1334003|lt=Saikaidō}}) were sent to the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu (Japan)}}.  

The '''{{ill|Kintō Maki|ja|近都牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintō Maki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諸国牧|1=Shokoku Maki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632062}}''' were pastures set up to allow these tribute horses and cattle to graze freely. These were primarily located in {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu}} and around the {{ill|Kinai|ja|近畿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近畿地方|1=Kansai region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q164256}} region.

Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, each pasture was managed by:
- One '''{{ill|Makichō|ja|牧長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (chief overseer of the pasture),
- One '''{{ill|Makichō (scribe)|ja|牧帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makichō (scribe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (scribe responsible for administrative records),
- Two '''{{ill|Makiko|ja|牧子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (caretakers) for every 100 horses or cattle.

The {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Provincial governor}} supervised these officials.  
At the central government level, the {{ill|Hyōbashi|ja|兵馬司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbashi|1=Hyōbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393722}} oversaw all pastures, both state-owned and private. Horses deemed suitable for riding were sent to military garrisons, where wealthier {{ill|Soldier|en|Soldier|WD=Q4991371|lt=soldiers}} cared for them, while lower-quality horses were used as relay station mounts. This system ensured state control over the supply and management of cavalry horses.  

If pasture officials (Makichō, Makichō scribes, and Makiko caretakers) allowed the horses to become emaciated, lost animals, or failed to meet the mandated increase of 60 foals per 100 breeding mares, they were punished. Conversely, those who exceeded the quota were rewarded with rice.

== Pastures and Locations ==

=== Shokoku Maki (Provincial Pastures) ===

* '''{{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}'''

* '''{{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|en|Awa Province (Chiba)|WD=Q1053044|lt=Awa Province (Japan)}}'''  
** {{ill|Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja|白浜馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahama Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kozu Horse Pasture|ja|粉師馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozu Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''{{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}'''  
** {{ill|Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|高野馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takano Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''{{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}}'''  
** {{ill|Okano Horse Pasture|ja|岡野馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okano Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Somi Horse Pasture|ja|蘇弥馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somi Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''{{ill|Bizen Province|en|Bizen Province|WD=Q1053688|lt=Bizen Province}}'''  
** {{ill|Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja|長嶋馬牛牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagashima Horse and Cattle Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Kintō Maki (Capital-Adjacent Pastures) ===
* '''{{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}'''  
** {{ill|Torigai Pasture|ja|鳥養牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torigai Pasture|1=Torigai Pasture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60986435}}, {{ill|Toyoshima Pasture|ja|豊島牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshima Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Inano Pasture|ja|為奈野牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inano Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''{{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}'''

* '''{{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province}}'''  
** {{ill|Goma Pasture|ja|胡麻牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goma Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Pastures and Locations ==

=== Shokoku Maki (Provincial Pastures) ===
* '''{{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}'''  
** {{ill|Shumon Horse Pasture|ja|朱門馬牧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shumon Horse Pasture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* '''{{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}'''
